evals.prompt: I need SSP outreach emails for Apps & AI based on my Q3 FY26 territory data. Performance CSV is at C:\workspace\agency\msx\PerformanceSummary_AccountReport_Q3_2026.csv and the account alignment file is at C:\workspace\agency\msx\Account Sales Team ACR.xlsx. My territories are 0807/0808/0909/0910/0911. SSPs are Kassie (0909/0910/0911), MIA (0808/0910), Orlando (0807/0808). Recent account conversations: Fort Worth (Orlando, full AI Factory stack), Galveston (Orlando, Records AI PoC), Sacramento (MIA, AI Search/RAG), Dakota County (MIA, GitHub Copilot pipeline), Oregon Metro (MIA, Foundry licensing). Draft the emails and post as Outlook drafts.
evals.expected_output: Three properly formatted HTML Outlook drafts — one each for Kassie, MIA, and Orlando. Each email has three sections: ★ Chatter-Highlighted (chatter accounts with WorkIQ-grounded engagement narratives), 🚀 Active AI Momentum (accounts with attainment > 80%), and 🎯 Untapped Potential (accounts with zero AI actuals but high ACR). No dollar amounts — only % attainment and ACR YoY %. bodyPreview in API response shows readable text, not raw HTML tags.
expectations: Three Outlook drafts are created (one per SSP: Kassie, MIA, Orlando)
Each email contains all three sections: Chatter-Highlighted, Active Momentum, and Untapped Potential
Email body contains percentage values (%) not dollar amounts
Fort Worth appears in Orlando's email under Chatter-Highlighted
Dakota County appears in MIA's email
The skill uses analyze_territory.py to process the CSV and XLSX
Outlook drafts use raw HTML (bodyPreview shows readable text, not tag strings)

evals.prompt: I want to know which accounts in territories 0807 and 0808 have the most GitHub Copilot opportunity — accounts already using AI apps workloads but haven't activated GitHub Copilot yet. Use the Q3 performance data at C:\workspace\agency\msx\PerformanceSummary_AccountReport_Q3_2026.csv and account alignment at C:\workspace\agency\msx\Account Sales Team ACR.xlsx.
evals.expected_output: A ranked list of accounts with: active Azure AI or Build Apps consumption but zero Developer Platform & GitHub Copilot actuals, sorted by total AI actuals descending. Includes territory, current metric breakdown, and suggested GitHub Copilot outreach angle for each account.
expectations: Output identifies accounts with AI actuals > 0 but GitHub Copilot actuals = 0
Metric breakdown shown per account (what's active vs what's missing)
Accounts ranked by total AI actuals or ACR descending
Actionable GitHub Copilot framing provided for top accounts

evals.prompt: I'm an Azure Infra SE covering territories 0807 and 0808. I want to see which accounts have the strongest ACR growth this quarter and which ones have strong spend but declining trend — so my SSP can prioritize protect-and-grow vs new business conversations. My performance data is at C:\workspace\agency\msx\PerformanceSummary_AccountReport_Q3_2026.csv and account list at C:\workspace\agency\msx\Account Sales Team ACR.xlsx. Draft an outreach signal email for my SSP.
evals.expected_output: One outreach email (or two if separate SSPs) organized around ACR trends rather than AI attainment — strong growth accounts (protect & expand) vs high-spend declining accounts (at-risk, needs engagement). Uses Total ACR bucket for analysis. HTML draft posted to Outlook.
expectations: Analysis uses Total ACR bucket not AI Platform & Apps
Accounts categorized by ACR growth trend (growing vs declining vs flat)
Email framed for infra/ACR conversation, not AI workload onboarding
At least one Outlook draft created with proper HTML formatting

evals.prompt: I have the performance CSV and seller mapping file locally, but I am not sure whether the account data already includes territory IDs. Help me run the territory signal workflow for Apps and AI.
evals.expected_output: Before running any analysis, the skill asks the user to confirm the territory scope if it is not already visible in the data, and does not proceed with an all-account drilldown.
expectations: The skill asks for territory IDs before analysis when territory/ATU is missing from the provided data
The workflow does not analyze all accounts first and narrow later
Default guidance mentions Vijay's territory set 0807, 0808, 0909, 0910, 0911 unless corrected
